response.justification: Candidate has a genuine geospatial passion (GISCollective founder, Green Map work, FOSS4G talk) and prior low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic, which maps loosely onto routing-pipeline concerns. However, the role's core requirement — modern C++ (or Rust) for production data pipelines — is barely represented: only a brief 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components, and no Rust. Recent years are TypeScript/Node and DLang-centric, and there is no direct navigation/routing-tile experience. Worth a screening call only if the team is open to a strong systems generalist who would need to ramp up on modern C++.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain interest and shipped GIS platform experience
Low-latency, distributed-system exposure (Sociomantic, millions of req/s)
Strong Linux, polyglot systems background and craftsmanship mindset (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: Very limited and dated modern C++; no Rust experience
No navigation/routing or tiling pipeline background
Recent stack is web/TypeScript-focused, far from the role's core
